D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Thermochromism — Leuco Dye + Developer + Wax

a three-component system (leuco dye, weak acid developer, paraffin wax solvent) is coloured below the wax melt and colourless above — the basis of every “mood ring”

A leuco dye like crystal violet lactone (CVL) is colourless on its own. Mixed with a phenolic developer (e.g. bisphenol A) it protonates to a deep-blue carbocation. A paraffin wax matrix holds them together only while solid; above the wax melt point (~30 °C for "mood ring" formulations) the components separate, the dye deprotonates, the colour vanishes. Recrystallisation on cooling restores the colour.

Drag the temperature across the melt point. The swatch flips from coloured-solid to colourless-liquid; the sharpness of the transition reflects the wax's sharp solid-liquid transition.
